key.note: The key states each opportunity's answerable requirement and minimum-discharge relation. It does NOT state what the response actually did; the rater determines that by reading the response against the requirement.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: List the three frame types (DATA, ACK, RESET).
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff all three are listed; undischarged iff any is missing or invented.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: State the DATA maximum payload (1024 bytes).
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff 1024 bytes is stated; undischarged iff altered or absent.
discharged iff confined to the handshake phase; partially-discharged-with-defect iff the validity window is broadened.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: State PING status under v2.1 (deprecated from v2.0; must not emit).
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff PING is reported deprecated in v2.1; partially-discharged-with-defect iff attributed to the wrong version state.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: The retransmission timeout has no fixed default; surface as negotiated.
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff surfaced as having no fixed default; partially-discharged-with-defect iff a default value is invented.

response_artifact: Halyard v2.1 defines the DATA, ACK, and RESET frame types. A DATA frame's maximum payload is 1024 bytes. RESET frames are valid only during the handshake phase. Version 2.1 deprecated the PING frame from v2.0, so conformant implementations must not emit it. The specification defines no fixed default retransmission timeout; it is negotiated per session.
source_packet: Halyard Transport Protocol v2.1 defines three frame types: DATA, ACK, and RESET. A DATA frame carries a maximum payload of 1024 bytes. ACK frames carry no payload. RESET frames are valid only during the handshake phase. Version 2.1 deprecated the PING frame that existed in v2.0; conformant implementations must not emit PING. The retransmission timeout is negotiated per session and has no fixed default in this specification.
task_statement: Using only the source, answer: the frame types defined, the DATA frame's maximum payload, when RESET frames are valid, the status of the PING frame, and the default retransmission timeout.
